commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a Telegram dispatch rotation at tool start plus regression tests so already-sent in-flight preamble text is preserved before resumed post-tool answer text.

PR surface: Source +22, Tests +191. Total +213 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: Source-reproducible: current main has no `onToolStart` answer-lane rotation, and post-tool partial text can reuse the mutable draft when no assistant boundary fires. I did not run tests because this review is constrained to read-only inspection.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is real behavior proof and maintainer review for a visible Telegram transport change, not a narrow automated code repair.

target.title: fix(telegram): rotate in-flight answer preamble at tool boundary
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes a Telegram bug where an **in-flight answer preamble is overwritten instead of preserved** when a tool runs mid-stream and post-tool text resumes.

When the model streams a short preamble (e.g. *"Let me actually check the current state of this file."*), the draft stream sends a Telegram message but leaves the lane **unfinalized**. If a `tool_use` then starts before any fresh assistant-message boundary (no changed `streamItemId` / `message_start`) and tool progress is suppressed (the default in partial streaming), the post-tool answer text keeps **editing the preamble bubble** instead of starting a new message. The user sees the preamble replaced by the post-tool text.

Root cause: rotation across the tool boundary only happened in two paths, both of which miss this case:
- `rotateFinalizedStream` only rotates a **finalized** lane — the preamble is still in-flight.
- `prepareAnswerLaneForToolProgress` only rotates when tool progress **actually renders** — suppressed in partial mode.

Reproduced at the dispatch level with the real `createTelegramDraftStream`, counting `sendRichMessage` (new message) vs `editMessageText` (edit-in-place): the in-flight case produced `sends: 1 / edits: 1` instead of the expected `sends: 2 / edits: 0`.

## Fix

Add `rotateInFlightAnswerPreambleForToolBoundary`, enqueued on the draft-lane event queue at `onToolStart` (so it is ordered after any pending preamble partials). It rotates the in-flight preamble into a fresh message only when it was actually sent and is still mutable:

- `hasStreamedMessage && !finalized && typeof stream.messageId() === "number"` — never rotates an empty or never-sent draft, and
- skips when `activeAnswerDraftIsToolProgressOnly` so it defers to the existing tool-progress rotation (no double-rotate).

Production change is +22 lines in `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts`.

## Verification

- Targeted (`bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`, `lane-delivery.test.ts`, deliverer contract test): **165 passed**. The decisive repro fails before the fix (`sends: 1 / edits: 1`) and passes after (`sends: 2 / edits: 0`); a control with a fresh assistant boundary passes both ways.
- Full Telegram extension suite: **2085 passed**, **2 pre-existing failures** in `bot-message-context.body.test.ts` (cached-sticker-description ~30s timeout falling back to `<media:image>`). These are unrelated to this change — that module imports none of the changed files and is on the inbound-body path, not outbound dispatch.
- Typechecks clean: `pnpm tsgo:extensions` and `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`.
- Clean two-commit bisect: failing reproduction first, then the fix.

---
*Authored by Sancho Panza, an AI squire acting on behalf of Kristof Csillag.*
target.author: sancho-csillag
